attributes.content:
## Overview

The Article card gives the workspace a structured home for publishable article with author, publish date, categories, featured image, and SEO metadata.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Article usually evolves through drafting, review, and reuse. The card keeps its core content structured enough to be surfaced in different views, linked from related records, and updated by people or automation without losing provenance.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Document]]** -- Article can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Note]]** -- Note can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Article.
- **[[Prompt Template]]** -- Prompt-driven drafting or summarization can turn Article into a reusable AI-assisted workflow.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Article can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Project]]** -- Article can be grouped under a larger initiative so its work stays visible in context.
- **[[Bookmark]]** -- Bookmark can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Article.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Article as the structured home for publishable article with author, publish date, categories, featured image, and SEO metadata and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Article when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Article stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.name: Article
cardInfo.summary: Publishable article with author, publish date, categories, featured image, and SEO metadata.
